completeness: AC IDs missing scenarios: AC-1.3, AC-3.6, AC-4.5, AC-5.6, AC-6.4

Step definitions in steps.ts cover only catalog.feature and add_entities.feature. Lending, returns, reservations, and fines features have no custom step definitions (e.g. 'Given a member "Alice" with ID "m1" is active', 'When the librarian lends the book to the member', 'Then the system creates a loan' all missing). Completeness capped at 2 per rubric rule.
alignment: Every scenario carries an @AC-x.y tag (e.g. @AC-3.1 on 'Successfully lend a book to a member', @AC-4.3 on 'Reject return of already returned book', @AC-5.3 on 'Reject duplicate reservation')

Each scenario maps to exactly one AC. Scenario names are human-readable restatements: 'Reject loan when member has outstanding fines exceeding limit', 'Return creates notification for next reservation', 'Expire stale reservations'
declarative: Good declarative: 'When the librarian lends the book to the member' (lending.feature:13), 'Then the loan is marked as returned' (returns.feature:13), 'Then the oldest waiting reservation becomes notified' (returns.feature:42), 'Then the fine is marked as paid' (fines.feature:18)

Bad implementation-level: 'Then the system returns a 409 error' (add_entities.feature:19, lending.feature:22, returns.feature:35, reservations.feature:19 etc.), 'Then the system returns a 404 error' (catalog.feature:25), 'Then the system returns a 403 error' (lending.feature:29), 'Then the system returns a 402 error' (lending.feature:36)
atomic: Strictly one action per step: 'When the librarian lends the book to the member' then 'Then the system creates a loan' then 'And sets the due date to 14 days from now' then 'And decreases the available copies by 1' (lending.feature:13-16)
And used to chain separate steps, never as a comma inside one step
